revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hitsuji-jinja (Nagoya)]][[ja:羊神社 (名古屋市)]][[sv:Hitsuji Jinja]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Hitsuji Shrine (Nagoya) | native_name = 羊神社 | image = Hitsujijinja3.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hitsuji Shrine (Nagoya) | coordinates = {{coord|35.20744444444444|136.92644444444446}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|az|Kaqutsuçi|de|Kagutsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|es|Kagutsuchi|fa|کاگو-تسوچی|fr|Kagutsuchi|it|Kagutsuchi|ja|カグツチ|mni|ꯀꯒꯨ-ꯁꯨꯆꯤ|pl|Kagutsuchi|ru|Кагуцути|sv|Kagutsuchi|ta|ககுட்சூசி|tr|Kagutsuchi|uk|Кагу-цуті|zh|迦具土|qid=Q1640658|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hitsuji Shrine (Nagoya)'''|羊神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province|ja|尾張国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} ** Series ordinal: 85 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039528|ltq=Q135039528|qid=Q135039528|1=Ohomeno Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Fukagawa Shrine|lt=Fukagawa Shrine|ja|深川神社|qid=Q11560019|1=Fukagawa Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1207964|ltq=Q1207964|qid=Q1207964|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}} * {{ill|Q514506|ltq=Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} * {{ill|Yamada district, Owari|lt=Yamada district, Owari|ja|山田郡 (尾張国)|zh|山田郡 (尾張國)|qid=Q11470699|1=Yamada district, Owari}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.20744444444444|136.92644444444446}} == image (P18) == * Hitsujijinja3.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q1640658|ltq=Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181425 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Son-sha|lt=Son-sha|ja|村社|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9180005001595 == Commons category (P373) == * Hitsuji-jinja (Nagoya) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Hitsushino Shrine|lt=Hitsushino Shrine|qid=Q135039529|1=Hitsushino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県名古屋市北区辻町5丁目26番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hitsuji-jinja (Nagoya)}} [[JA:羊神社 (名古屋市)]] [[SV:Hitsuji Jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q22120105}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Hitsujinja Shrine |Image = [[File:Hitsujijinja3.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya City|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|qid=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}} Tsujimachi 5-chōme 26 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Hi no Kagutsuchi no Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}{{sfn|Aichi Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines|1992|p=3}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{sfn|Aichi Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines|1992|p=3}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}{{sfn|Aichi Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines|1992|p=3}} |Alternate names = Shinmei
Tsujimura Shinmei |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|qid=Q2964|1=October 18}}{{sfn|Aichi Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines|1992|p=3}} |Rituals = {{ill|lt=Sagichō|Q9332740|qid=Q9332740|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} ({{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|qid=Q2260|1=January 15}}){{sfn|Aichi Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines|1992|p=3}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Hitsujinja Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Hitsujinja Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hitsujinja Shrine''' (ひつじじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya City|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|qid=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Tsujimachi (Nagoya)|Q17215607|qid=Q17215607|lt=Tsujimachi|1=Tsujichō}}. It is identified with the “Hitsujinja Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Yamada District (Owari Province)|Q11470699|qid=Q11470699|lt=Yamada District|1=Yamada district, Owari}}. === Overview === Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|qid=Q184963}}, it was called '''Shinmei''' and served as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|qid=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} (guardian shrine) of {{ill|lt=Kasugai District|Q11513576|qid=Q11513576|1=Kasugai district}}{{ill|lt=Tsujimachi (Nagoya)|Q17215607|qid=Q17215607|lt=Tsujimura|1=Tsujichō}}. The year of foundation is unknown, but as a {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|qid=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridge tag) from the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era remains, it is presumed to date to before the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}. The early Edo-period document '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|qid=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}' records it as “Tsujimura... Shinmei, previously exempt.” It was identified by {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|qid=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} and others as the “Hitsujinja Shrine” of Yamada District in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and this has been the prevailing theory since the Edo period {{Sfn|Aichi Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines|1992|p=3}}. The basis for this identification is an old tradition stating that because the location was Tsujimura, and “Hitsuji” (sheep) was written as “Fire Tsujī,” the character “fire” was taken to form the place name “Tsujī” {{sfn|Aichi Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines|1992|p=3}}. However, {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|qid=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}}, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=local historian|1=UNKNOWN}} of the late Edo period, proposed an alternative theory that the original Hitsujinja Shrine was the Tenjin of {{ill|lt=Seko (Nagoya)|Q29902337|qid=Q29902337|lt=Seko Village|1=Seko}} (present-day Takamuta Shrine). Records indicate that the shrine buildings were completed in August of {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|qid=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1838|Q7612|qid=Q7612|1=1838}}) {{sfn|Aichi Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines|1992|p=3}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5, it was designated a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} {{sfn|Aichi Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines|1992|p=3}}. It has been worshipped as a deity for protection against fire, and it is said that even during {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|qid=Q362|1=World War II}}, parishioners were spared from destruction by air raids {{sfn|Aichi Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines|1992|p=3}}. It is also said that the shrine’s name derives from a residence once located here that belonged to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tago Hitsujidayū|1=UNKNOWN}}, who ruled {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|qid=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{ill|lt=Tago District|Q11431032|qid=Q11431032|1=Tago District, Gunma}} and stopped here when traveling to the capital at Nara . === Subsidiary shrines === * Hakusan Shrine * Akiba Shrine * Tsushima Shrine * Ontake Shrine * Yama Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Inari Shrine === Gallery === {{gallery |File:Hitsujijinja1.JPG|Torii |File:Hitsujijinja4.JPG|Temizuya |File:Hitsujijinja2.JPG| }} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist|refs= {{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www1.s3.starcat.ne.jp/hituji-m/yurai.html |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160307213048/http://www1.s3.starcat.ne.jp/hituji-m/yurai.html |title=Shikinaisha Hitsujinja Shrine: Brief History of Its Origins|publisher=Hitsujinja Shrine|language=Japanese |archivedate=2016-03-07 |accessdate=2019-05-03}} }} === References === * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Aichi Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines|title=Directory of Shinto Shrines in Aichi Prefecture|publisher=Aichi Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines|date=1992-08|language=Japanese|id={{National Bibliography Number|93018567}}|ref=harv}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Hitsujinja Shrine (Annaka City)|Q63437013|qid=Q63437013|1=Hitsuji Shrine}} === External links === {{osm box|w|512843717}} * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kita/page/0000000918.html Nagoya City: Hitsujinja Shrine (Kita Ward)] * {{Wayback|http://www1.s3.starcat.ne.jp:80/hituji-m/ |title=Hitsujinja Shrine Nagoya |date=20160625182458 }} * {{commonscat-inline}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:History of Kita ward]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita ward]]
